title: Webhook Test Delivery and Auto-Disable

story.i_want_to: send a test event to a webhook and have webhooks auto-disabled after too many consecutive failures, with visibility into recent delivery attempts
story.so_that: I can verify my webhook endpoint works before relying on it, broken webhooks stop wasting delivery resources, and I can diagnose delivery issues

rationale: Without a test endpoint, tenants must trigger a real state change to verify their webhook is configured correctly — an error-prone and inconvenient workflow. Auto-disable prevents wasted Oban worker time on permanently broken endpoints (e.g., decommissioned servers). The deliveries listing gives tenants visibility into what was delivered, what failed, and why, enabling self-service debugging.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/webhooks/:id/test creates a webhook_event with event_type='webhook.test' and immediately enqueues it for delivery via the Oban worker. The test payload includes {event: 'webhook.test', data: {message: 'This is a test event', webhook_id: id}, timestamp: ISO8601}.
The test endpoint returns 200 OK with the created webhook_event ID. Delivery happens asynchronously — the response does not wait for delivery to complete.
Test events follow the same signing and delivery path as real events (HMAC-SHA256 signature, same headers, same retry logic).
When a webhook's consecutive_failures reaches the tenant's webhook_max_consecutive_failures setting (default 10), the webhook is automatically set to active=false.
Auto-disable is checked after each failed delivery exhaustion. The check uses the tenant's configurable threshold, not a hardcoded value.
When a webhook is auto-disabled, an audit log entry is created with action='webhook_auto_disabled', including the consecutive_failures count and the last error message.

GET /api/v1/webhooks/:id/deliveries returns recent webhook_event records for the specified webhook, ordered by inserted_at descending. Includes: id, event_type, status, attempts, delivered_at, error, inserted_at. Default page size 25, max 100.
A manually re-activated webhook (PATCH active=true) resets consecutive_failures to 0.
All three endpoints (test, deliveries, PATCH for reactivation) require the user role. Tenant-scoped via RLS.
The test endpoint works even if the webhook is currently inactive (active=false), allowing tenants to test before reactivating.

test_cases.name: test endpoint creates and enqueues test event
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Response includes the created webhook_event ID
webhook_event record exists with event_type='webhook.test' and status='pending'
An Oban job is enqueued for the WebhookDeliveryWorker

test_cases.name: test event is signed like real events
expected_results: X-Signature-256 header is present
Signature is valid HMAC-SHA256 of the payload using the webhook's signing secret
X-Webhook-ID and X-Webhook-Timestamp headers are present

test_cases.name: auto-disable after consecutive failures threshold
preconditions: fixture(:webhook_event, %{webhook_id: webhook.id, tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :pending, attempts: 5})
HTTP mock returns 500 for all attempts
expected_results: webhook_event status is 'exhausted'
webhook consecutive_failures is 3 (matches threshold)
webhook active is false (auto-disabled)
Audit log entry exists with action='webhook_auto_disabled'

test_cases.name: auto-disable respects tenant-specific threshold
expected_results: webhook is still active (threshold is 20, not the default 10)
consecutive_failures is 10

test_cases.name: deliveries endpoint returns recent delivery attempts
preconditions: 5 webhook_event records for the webhook with mixed statuses (delivered, failed, exhausted, pending)
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
Returns 5 delivery records ordered by inserted_at descending
Each record includes id, event_type, status, attempts, delivered_at, error, inserted_at
Payload field is NOT included in the deliveries list (to reduce response size)

test_cases.name: reactivation resets consecutive_failures
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
webhook active is true
webhook consecutive_failures is 0

test_cases.name: test endpoint works on inactive webhook
expected_results: Response is 200 OK
webhook_event is created with event_type='webhook.test'
Oban job is enqueued (test events bypass the active check)

test_cases.name: tenant isolation on deliveries endpoint
preconditions: webhook_events exist for Tenant B's webhook
steps: GET /api/v1/webhooks/<tenant_b_webhook_id>/deliveries with Tenant A's API key
expected_results: Response is 404 Not Found (webhook not visible to Tenant A via RLS)

technical_notes: POST /api/v1/webhooks/:id/test route in WebhookController. Creates a webhook_event and inserts an Oban job in one transaction.
GET /api/v1/webhooks/:id/deliveries route in WebhookController (or a nested WebhookDeliveryController). Queries webhook_events for the webhook.
Auto-disable logic lives in the WebhookDeliveryWorker after exhaustion: load tenant settings, compare consecutive_failures >= threshold, update webhook active=false if exceeded.
When PATCH sets active=true and the webhook's current consecutive_failures > 0, the update changeset should also reset consecutive_failures to 0.
Test events should bypass the normal active check in the delivery worker. Add a flag in the worker args or check event_type='webhook.test'.
The deliveries endpoint should exclude the full payload from the list response for performance. Include a link or note that full payload is available via the audit log.
Add webhook_max_consecutive_failures to the tenant settings schema with a default of 10.
